data.title: Uniswap DAO Contributor Resigns Over Power Concerns
data.description:
According to Foresight News, a leading contributor to the Uniswap DAO, known as Pepo, has resigned due to concerns over the concentration of power among other stakeholders, particularly the Uniswap Foundation. Pepo expressed that the actions of the Uniswap Foundation prioritize the DAO's interests over those of the Foundation itself and Uniswap Labs. 

Pepo, an anonymous representative, has been involved in Uniswap's governance since 2023, representing other token holders in voting matters. He holds 455,000 UNI tokens, making him one of the top 20 largest representatives.

Devin Walsh, the Executive Director of the Uniswap Foundation, emphasized the importance of representative participation for the success of the Uniswap ecosystem. Walsh stated that the Foundation highly values the feedback from these representatives.

data.title: Arbitrum Community Considers Lowering Voting Threshold Amid Governance Crisis
data.description:
According to PANews, the governance token ARB of Ethereum's Layer 2 network, Arbitrum, has experienced a significant price drop of 71% over the past year, leading to a sharp decline in DAO voting participation. In response to this challenge, the Arbitrum community is contemplating reducing the minimum voting threshold for proposal approval from 5% to 4.5% to revitalize governance activity. This move comes amid a growing governance participation crisis, with data indicating a 50% decrease in Arbitrum's governance engagement over the past year, alongside an 87% drop in token price from its historical peak.

Community representatives have noted that when token holders face substantial losses, their motivation to engage in governance naturally diminishes. This situation not only affects routine decision-making but also risks preventing quality proposals from passing. Some representatives support lowering the threshold as a temporary measure, but the core issue remains the ecosystem's lack of tangible value to retain token holders.

data.title: The Arbitrum community is considering lowering the minimum voting threshold for proposals to pass from 5% to 4.5%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Arbitrum community is considering lowering the minimum voting threshold required for proposals to pass from 5% to 4.5% to revitalize governance. This move stems from the increasingly serious governance participation crisis. Data shows that Arbitrum's governance participation has fallen by 50% in the past year, and its token price has fallen by 87% from its historical high. (DL News)

data.title: Coinbase Disclosure Documents Show New York Attorney General Asked the US SEC to Classify ETH as a Security
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase recently obtained and disclosed a batch of internal communication documents of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) through the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), which showed that the New York Attorney General's Office had asked the U.S. SEC to clearly identify Ethereum (ETH) as a security during the lawsuit against KuCoin in 2023.
Shamiso Maswoswe, director of the New York Investor Protection Bureau, said in a letter that he hoped the U.S. SEC would submit a friend of the court opinion to support the view that ETH is a security to strengthen investor protection. However, the U.S. SEC ultimately did not adopt the request, but the documents revealed the regulator's internal discussions on the characterization of ETH, especially after Ethereum switched to the PoS consensus mechanism. Coinbase said the disclosure is intended to increase regulatory transparency and promote public understanding of crypto asset regulatory policies. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitcoin Payment App Strike to Launch BTC Lending Service Soon
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bitcoin payment application Strike is about to enter the BTC lending business service Strike Lending. The company's CEO Jack Mallers revealed that Strike plans to provide users with a way to borrow fiat currency while continuing to hold Bitcoin. It is reported that Strike Lending will initially be launched in parts of the United States and plans to expand internationally. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Withholds Details on Potential Trade Agreements
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has declined to disclose which countries the United States might reach trade agreements with this week. Besant stated that revealing such information could harm U.S. interests.

data.title: Bitcoin Traders Prepare for Potential Downtrend Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description: According to Odaily, Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks.live, shared insights in an English community briefing, highlighting a bearish sentiment among group members. Traders are preparing for a possible downward trend, with several members closely monitoring Bitcoin's price range between $93,000 and $99,000. The current market volatility has led to a lack of enthusiasm among participants.

data.title: Former Trump cryptocurrency policy adviser plans to set up a Bitcoin investment company and intends to raise $300 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to people familiar with the matter, David Bailey, who served as Donald Trump's cryptocurrency policy adviser during the 2024 campaign, is raising $300 million to establish a listed Bitcoin investment company, trying to replicate the success of Michael Saylor's Strategy, which has transformed into a Bitcoin investment company and is raising funds through private placement of new shares and convertible bonds. (The Information)

data.title: Binance Wallet Launches BNB Smart Chain Trading Competition on Binance Alpha
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Binance Wallet launched the BNB Smart Chain Trading Competition on Binance Alpha. Trading B2, AIOT, MYX and ZKJ in Binance Wallet (without private key) or through Binance Alpha can share token rewards. The event time is: 23:00 on May 7, 2025 to 00:00 on May 22, 2025 (Eastern Time Zone 8). Participants will be ranked according to four independent categories: 
B2 Trading Volume Pool: The top 5,000 users in terms of B2 token trading volume during the event will share 883,000 B2 tokens (= 176 B2 per user). 
AIOT Trading Volume Pool: The top 10,000 users in terms of AIOT token trading volume during the event will share 2,306,000 AIOT tokens (= 230 AIOT per user). 
MYX Volume Pool: The top 5,000 users in terms of MYX token trading volume during the event will share 5,117,000 MYX tokens (= 1,023 MYX per user).
ZKJ Volume Pool: The top 20,000 users in terms of ZKJ token trading volume during the event will share 700,000 ZKJ tokens (= 35 ZKJ per user).
Please note:
Only transactions through Binance Wallet (no private key) or Binance Alpha are eligible for this event, third-party dApp transactions are not included.
No Volume Cap: There is no upper limit on the trading volume per participant in this event.
The rewards in this event are not mutually exclusive, and eligible winners can receive rewards from each trading task pool if they meet the above criteria.
Transactions related to cross-chain bridges are not counted towards the trading volume of this event.
